categoryDescription: Looks at how the ETF is positioned for the next 6-12 months and the 3-5 year secular path across four/five forward-looking lenses: underlying valuation + fundamentals, macro regime fit (short + long horizon), cycle position of the exposure, and (for rate-sensitive / income funds) the yield + rate + credit outlook. Two group-specific factors handle exposures that don't fit the generic lenses: `commodity_supply_demand_or_adoption_outlook` for commodities and digital assets, and `leveraged_holding_window_outlook` for leveraged / inverse products. This category is forward-looking but NOT predictive — each factor asks 'is the fund favorably positioned given the regime and expected path?', not 'what's the price target?'. The prompt expects and encourages web lookup of macro indicators, valuation multiples, rate-path expectations, credit spreads, sector flows, vol regime, and upcoming policy / earnings catalysts — provided data blocks alone are insufficient. Each group uses exactly 3 factors. `factorDescription` carries the generic measurement principle and Pass/Fail rule; `groupInstructions` carries the group-specific perspective.

factors.factorDescription: Two reads in one factor: where do the fund's underlying holdings sit on valuation vs their own 5-10y history, AND what's the fundamental trajectory of those holdings? For equity funds: forward P/E, P/B, P/S, dividend yield, P/E-to-growth versus the index's own historical range, combined with earnings-revisions trend, margin direction, and free-cash-flow momentum over the past 1-3 quarters. For fixed-income funds: SEC yield vs its own 5-10y range, real yield (SEC yield minus breakeven inflation) vs historical norm, spreads vs 10y median (for credit), combined with upgrade/downgrade ratio and default-rate trend. For allocation / target-date funds: composite of the equity sleeve's valuation + earnings trajectory and the bond sleeve's yield + credit-quality trajectory, weighted by mix. The four-quadrant decision frame: cheap + improving = best setup; cheap + deteriorating = value-trap risk; expensive + improving = momentum, defendable; expensive + deteriorating = worst, expects multiple compression. Valuation alone is a framing tool, not a market-timing tool — markets stay expensive for long periods — but combined with the trajectory it tells retail how much margin of error they have. Pass when valuation is at or below category median AND fundamentals are flat-to-improving, OR valuations are rich but fundamentals are clearly strong enough to defend them. Fail when valuations sit in the top decile of own history AND fundamentals are deteriorating — the next 12 months will likely need multiple compression.
factors.factorMetrics: indexName, overviewCategory, peRatio, dividendYield, overviewSecYield, duration, topHoldings, sectorBreakdown, strategyText

groupInstructions.broad-equity: Forward P/E vs the index's own 5-10y range — S&P 500 historical median is roughly 17x trailing / 16x forward; top decile of own history is a yellow flag. Combine with earnings-revisions trend (FactSet bottom-up consensus) over the past 1-3 quarters — rising revisions and expanding margins defend rich valuations; falling revisions on a high P/E is the worst combination. For style tilts, compare to the style sub-index's own history (Russell 1000 Growth vs Value).
groupInstructions.sector-thematic-equity: Sector P/E vs the sector's own 10y range — technology 18-35x, financials 10-15x, staples 18-22x, energy is reserves-driven so cycle-adjust. Combine with EPS revisions specific to the sector — semiconductors mid-cycle EPS reversion, energy cap-ex cycle, financials NIM. For thematic funds, also flag adoption-curve maturity (early thematic + improving fundamentals = best; late thematic + decelerating adoption = bubble risk).
groupInstructions.derivative-income: Underlying equity index valuation matters because the strategy writes options ON that index — JEPI / SPYI write on S&P 500, QYLD / JEPQ on Nasdaq-100. Read the underlying's forward P/E vs history and earnings trajectory; rich valuations + decelerating earnings make the upside-capped strategy harder (less upside to give up, distributions lean on premium harvested in lower vol).
groupInstructions.allocation-target-date: Composite of equity-sleeve P/E + earnings trajectory AND bond-sleeve SEC yield + credit trajectory, weighted by mix. A 60/40 at 17x P/E on stocks and 5% on bonds in a rate-cycle peak is differently positioned than the same fund at 22x P/E and 3% on bonds. Frame the composite.
groupInstructions.fixed-income-investment-grade: SEC yield vs its own 5-10y range AND real yield (SEC yield minus breakeven inflation) vs historical norm. For muni, quote tax-equivalent yield at ~32% federal vs comparable taxable bonds. Credit-quality trajectory is a flag — a 'core' fund holding 30%+ BBB is reaching for yield. Carry compensation: at current SEC yield and current duration, does the carry cover a reasonable rate move? Long-duration funds with low real yield (<0.5%) carry asymmetric downside.
groupInstructions.fixed-income-credit-and-income: Spreads (HY OAS, IG credit spread, EM debt spread) vs 10y median plus SEC yield vs own range. Tight spreads in the bottom quintile of history = thin compensation for default risk; wide spreads in the top quintile + improving cycle = best entry. Combine with default-rate forecast (Moody's HY default forecast) and upgrade/downgrade ratio. Carry-only late-cycle is acceptable; spread-tight + late-cycle + rising defaults is Fail.

factors.factorTitle: Macro Regime Fit — Short and Long Horizon
factors.factorDescription: Looks at whether the fund's exposure profile fits the current and expected macro regime over TWO horizons: short (6-12 months — Fed stance, current CPI / PMI / unemployment / financial conditions, near-term catalysts like Fed meetings / CPI prints / OPEC+ decisions / elections that could meaningfully reprice the fund) AND long (3-5 years and beyond — secular regime and structural trends like deglobalization, AI build-out, energy transition, demographics, debt-to-GDP trajectory). The macro frame has four axes retail can grasp: growth (expanding / slowing / contracting), inflation (rising / sticky / falling), monetary policy (tightening / holding / easing), and financial conditions (easy / tight). Each fund type has a preferred regime — growth and large-cap tech do well in easy-money + falling-inflation + expanding-growth setups; value and dividend equity do better in rising-rate + sticky-inflation; long-duration Treasuries rally in falling-rate + slowing-growth; credit-risk assets need stable growth AND narrowing spreads; gold benefits from negative real rates + weakening dollar; digital assets favor easy financial conditions and risk-on appetite; managed-futures benefit from sustained trends; market-neutral needs dispersion. Name the current regime explicitly using available macro indicators AND name the expected secular trend. Pass when both horizons are favorable, OR one is favorable and the other is mixed-but-manageable. Fail when both horizons are working against the fund (e.g. long-duration Treasury fund in a re-accelerating inflation + hawkish-Fed setup with secular pressure on government issuance).
factors.factorMetrics: overviewCategory, assetClass, indexName, duration, beta, strategyText

groupInstructions.broad-equity: Short horizon: track Fed stance + CPI direction + employment trend; broad equity wants a soft-landing macro and is hurt by re-accelerating inflation or aggressive Fed tightening. Long horizon: US large-cap has been the structural winner since 2009 — secular pillars are AI productivity, energy transition, US labor productivity advantage. Foreign developed has cyclical mean-reversion case; EM is path-dependent on dollar and China growth.
groupInstructions.sector-thematic-equity: Short horizon: sector cycle position (tech leads early cycle; financials benefit from yield-curve steepening; energy rides commodity cycle; defensives outperform late-cycle). Long horizon: theme durability — AI / robotics / clean-energy / semis have 5-10y secular tailwinds; some themes (peak-oil, water, organic-food) have weaker structural cases. For single-country EM, name the country's 3-5y growth trajectory and currency stability.
groupInstructions.leveraged-inverse: Short horizon dominates (only the next 1-3 months is actually relevant for these products) — track upcoming Fed meetings, CPI prints, OPEC+ decisions, earnings windows; binary events drive gap moves that amplify by the leverage factor. Long-horizon view is essentially 'don't hold this that long' — say it explicitly. The macro question is 'does the next 1-3 months lean in the leverage direction or against it?'.
groupInstructions.commodities-and-digital-assets: Short horizon: USD trend, real-rate path, geopolitical risk-on / risk-off (gold loves negative real rates; oil tracks OPEC+ and global growth; BTC has correlated with risk-on / Nasdaq since 2022). Long horizon: gold has structural central-bank demand since 2022; oil has the energy-transition vs continued-demand tug; BTC has the adoption arc (halving cycle, ETF approval, treasury allocations); silver has industrial demand from EV / solar.
groupInstructions.derivative-income: Short horizon: vol regime determines option premium (low VIX = compressed yield; elevated VIX = rich premium). Long horizon: rate-cycle position + equity-cycle position together determine whether covered-call income can be sustainably re-extracted (sustained low vol + flat-up equities = QYLD's hardest setup). Name the regime and how it interacts with the option-writing strategy.
groupInstructions.allocation-target-date: Short horizon: rate path + equity multiple + recession probability — the 60/40 problem of 2022 was simultaneous rate shock + equity drawdown. Long horizon: 60/40 forward expected returns at current valuations run roughly 5-7% real depending on starting yields; secular drivers (productivity, demographics, debt) shape the long arc.
groupInstructions.fixed-income-investment-grade: Short horizon: market-implied rate path (Fed dot-plot vs SOFR / OIS-implied cuts and hikes) and yield-curve shape. Long horizon: secular pressure on Treasury issuance + fiscal trajectory + reserve-currency status; muni demand from the tax-bracket structure; TIPS for long-run inflation hedge.
groupInstructions.fixed-income-credit-and-income: Short horizon: credit-cycle stage + spread levels + default-rate trend (early cycle = best buy; late-cycle tight-spreads = thin compensation). Long horizon: secular default trend (HY defaults rising as the rate environment normalizes), private-credit growth changing public-credit dynamics, EM debt currency risk.

factors.factorTitle: Cycle Position of the Fund's Exposure
factors.factorDescription: Applies a four-phase cycle framework to the fund's specific exposure — the broad market for broad-equity, the sector / theme / country for sector-thematic-equity, the underlying index for leveraged-inverse, the specific commodity or digital asset for commodities-and-digital-assets. The four phases: (1) accumulation (underowned, improving fundamentals, rising relative strength, smart-money entering before consensus); (2) markup (trending, consensus building, valuations expanding, flows confirming); (3) distribution (consensus positive but breadth narrowing, flow momentum flagging, leadership rotating); (4) markdown (downtrending, outflows, capitulation setting up the next accumulation). Read price action (trend strength, breadth, relative strength vs the broader market), sentiment (positioning, AUM flows, narrative saturation), and fundamentals together. Hype-peak red flags for thematic / single-country / digital-asset wrappers: sudden AUM surge + narrative saturation + stretched valuations + breadth narrowing to a few names = late distribution. Pass when the exposure is in accumulation or early markup with fundamental support and improving flows. Fail when it's in late distribution (narrowing breadth, consensus crowded) or active markdown (deteriorating flows and fundamentals).
factors.factorMetrics: overviewCategory, indexName, sectorBreakdown, topHoldings, ma50, ma200, rsiM, athChgPercent, atlChgPercent, aum, strategyText

groupInstructions.broad-equity: Apply the cycle framework to the broad index the fund tracks (S&P 500, total US market, MSCI EAFE, MSCI ACWI). Markup phase: index above MA200, broad participation (>60% of S&P stocks above their own MA200), AAII bullish but not extreme. Late distribution: narrow breadth (mega-cap-led), top-decile valuations, crowded long flows. Markdown: index below MA200, weak breadth, AAII bearish extreme often near the bottom.
groupInstructions.sector-thematic-equity: Sector and thematic cycles run independently of the broad market. Read the sector's own MA20 / 50 / 200, weekly RSI, and relative strength vs the S&P 500. Thematic hype-peak signals: peak AUM in the same quarter as peak P/E, narrative saturation (theme on every cover), breadth narrow to a handful of names. For single-country EM, track local-index relative strength vs MSCI EM, currency stability, and foreign-investor flows.
groupInstructions.leveraged-inverse: Apply the cycle framework to the UNDERLYING index, not the leveraged product itself — the leveraged fund's price is purely a leverage transform of the underlying. Where is the underlying in its cycle? Accumulation / markup phases reward leveraged longs (and hurt inverse); distribution / markdown reward inverse (and hurt longs). State the underlying's cycle phase plainly and tie to the fund's direction.
groupInstructions.commodities-and-digital-assets: Apply the cycle framework to the specific underlying. Gold has long cycles tied to the real-rate regime — the current cycle has been markup since 2022 driven by central-bank buying. BTC follows the 4-year halving cycle (April 2024 halving → historical 12-18 month post-halving rally pattern); also overlay the adoption arc from spot-ETF approval. Oil has shorter cycles tied to OPEC+ and global demand (the late-2022 to 2024 range was distribution-to-markdown). Name the specific cycle being read.

factors.factorDescription: For rate-sensitive and income-focused funds, combines three reads in one factor: (a) market-implied rate path (Fed dot-plot vs SOFR / OIS-implied cuts and hikes, yield-curve shape) vs the fund's duration — long-duration funds (>10y) rally hard if rates fall ~100 bps and lose 10%+ if rates rise ~100 bps; short-duration (<3y) is relatively insulated; intermediate (5-7y) is balanced; floating-rate (bank loan, AAA CLO) is near-zero duration so benefits from sustained high short rates but loses coupon income when the Fed cuts; (b) credit-cycle stage and current spread levels vs 10y history — early-cycle (widening spreads + Fed cutting) is the strongest buy setup for credit; late-cycle (tight spreads + rising defaults) argues for carry-only exposure; (c) yield / distribution sustainability — is SEC yield close to distribution yield (healthy), or is distribution materially above SEC yield (likely ROC propping, not sustainable); for derivative-income funds, is the option-premium engine still working in the current vol regime. For non-income strategy funds (managed futures, market-neutral) where headline yield is zero by design, Pass by default with a one-line note that income is not the mandate. Pass when (i) duration matches or benefits from the market-implied path, AND (ii) credit spreads are at or above long-run median (or the fund is rates-only with no credit exposure), AND (iii) yield is mostly earned with sustainable composition. Fail when duration is clearly offside the implied rate path, OR spreads are in the tightest quintile with late-cycle deterioration, OR the headline yield is propped up by ROC / a compressing premium engine.
factors.factorMetrics: duration, overviewCategory, indexName, beta, riskAndVolatilityMeasures, overviewSecYield, overviewTtmYield, dividendYield, divYears, divGrYears, strategyText

groupInstructions.fixed-income-investment-grade: Pure-duration lens. Read the market-implied path (CME FedWatch, SOFR-implied curve) vs the fund's duration. Long-duration Treasury / muni / IG aggregate (10+y duration) is a directional rate bet; intermediate is balanced; ultrashort is cash-substitute. SEC yield should closely track distribution yield (large divergence signals smoothing or de-accumulated coupons). For muni, frame in TEY at ~32% federal. Quote real yield (SEC minus breakeven inflation) — long-duration with real yield <0.5% is asymmetric downside.
groupInstructions.fixed-income-credit-and-income: Credit-cycle lens dominates. Compare HY OAS, IG spreads, EM debt spreads to 10y medians — tight bottom quintile = thin compensation; wide top quintile + improving cycle = best buy. Read default-rate forecasts (Moody's HY default forecast) and bank-lending standards (SLOOS survey). For bank loans / AAA CLO, duration is near-zero but coupon resets — favorable in sustained high-rates, hostile in cut cycles. SEC yield should closely match distribution; ROC share on preferreds / convertibles can be material.
groupInstructions.derivative-income: Option-premium engine lens. Headline yield depends on the vol regime — low VIX (<15) compresses option premium (covered-call yield falls); elevated VIX (>20) rewards premium harvesting but the equity drawdown that caused it offsets total return. Distribution composition: JEPI is mostly qualified dividends + ELN income; QYLD-style funds often 40%+ ROC. Quote SEC vs distribution yield, ROC share, and a forward distribution range under low- and elevated-vol scenarios.
groupInstructions.allocation-target-date: The bond-sleeve duration drives overall rate sensitivity. A 60/40 with a 6-year-duration bond sleeve has ~2.4-year effective duration (6 × 0.4). Frame the rate-path implication: a 100 bps rate move → roughly 2.4% bond-sleeve hit. Equity-sleeve valuation pairs with this for the full rate-cycle read. Target-date glide paths near retirement are bond-heavy and rate-sensitive.

factors.factorDescription: For commodity and digital-asset funds, evaluates the supply-demand setup (for commodities) or adoption / regulatory setup (for crypto) over both horizons — next 6-12 months and the 3-5 year secular path. The relevant read varies by sub-type: precious metals = real-rate path + USD trend + central-bank buying + jewelry / industrial demand; energy = OPEC+ stance + US shale capacity + strategic-reserve activity + global growth + weather (for nat gas); broad commodities and agriculturals = China growth read (single biggest swing) + weather (El Niño / La Niña) + fertilizer prices + currency cycle of major exporters; spot crypto = spot-ETF flow trend + institutional adoption (treasury allocations) + regulatory path + halving cycle (BTC, April 2024 → historical 12-18 month post-halving rally pattern) + L2 / ecosystem activity (ETH, SOL); currency wrappers = rate differentials + central-bank divergence + terms of trade. Pass when the supply-demand or adoption setup is constructive over the relevant horizon (e.g. tight supply + improving demand for an industrial commodity, accelerating adoption + favorable regulatory path for crypto). Fail when oversupply or hostile regulation dominates without an offsetting demand pickup.
factors.factorMetrics: overviewCategory, assetClass, indexName, strategyText, aum, return1y, return3y, return5y

groupInstructions.commodities-and-digital-assets: Identify the sub-type first, then apply the right lens. **Precious metals (gold, silver, platinum)**: real-rate direction (gold loves falling real rates), USD trend (inverse), central-bank gold buying (structural since 2022), silver / platinum industrial demand from EV / solar / catalytic converters. **Energy (oil, natural gas)**: OPEC+ production stance, US shale break-even (~$50-60/bbl), strategic-reserve refill, global growth (especially China), winter heating for nat gas. **Broad commodities / agriculturals**: China growth, weather patterns (multi-year ENSO cycle), fertilizer prices, supply concentration in a few exporters. **Spot crypto BTC**: spot-ETF flow trend (IBIT / FBTC AUM trajectory), institutional treasury allocations, regulatory clarity (SEC posture), halving cycle (April 2024 halving → historical 12-18 month rally pattern). **Spot crypto ETH / SOL / XRP**: ETF approval status, staking yield, L2 / ecosystem activity, regulatory clarity. **Currency wrappers**: rate differential vs USD, central-bank policy divergence, terms-of-trade trajectory.

factors.factorDescription: For leveraged / inverse products, evaluates whether the expected market environment over the next 1-3 months — the realistic holding window for daily-reset products — favors compounding or punishes it. Three inputs together: (1) expected vol regime (current VIX vs realized vol, implied-vol skew, expected vol surprises from upcoming Fed / CPI / earnings windows); (2) trend persistence (is the underlying showing clean trend signals or is the tape choppy / mean-reverting); (3) binary-event clustering (Fed meetings, CPI / PCE / employment prints, OPEC+ decisions, options-expiry weeks, earnings windows for the underlying constituents). Clean trend + manageable vol + sparse binary events = compounding works for you. Chop + vol spikes + dense binary events = decay compounds against you regardless of direction. Distinct from `macro_regime_fit` (which says which direction) and `market_or_sector_cycle_position` (which says where in the cycle): this factor says whether the environment over the next few weeks is favorable for short-term leveraged trading at all. Pass when the expected window setup is favorable (clean directional trend + moderate vol + sparse events). Fail when the setup is hostile (choppy tape, elevated implied vol, dense binary-event clustering).
factors.factorMetrics: ma20, ma50, ma150, ma200, ma50ChgPercent, ma200ChgPercent, rsi, rsiW, rsiM, atr, beta, riskAndVolatilityMeasures, return1y, returnsAnnual, strategyText

groupInstructions.leveraged-inverse: Quote three things together: (a) current VIX or relevant vol index (DVOL for crypto, GVZ for gold, OVX for oil) vs its 90-day average — is vol elevated or compressed? (b) trend signals on the underlying (price vs MA20 / 50 / 200, weekly RSI, ADX-like trend strength) — is the tape trending or chopping? (c) upcoming binary events in the next 1-3 months (Fed meeting dates, CPI prints, OPEC+ meetings, earnings clusters for the underlying constituents). Combine: clean trend + moderate vol + sparse events = favorable window; chop OR elevated vol OR dense events = unfavorable. Reinforce: even in a favorable window, the holding horizon is days-to-weeks, never multi-month.
